title: Signature-domain multiplexing for non-orthogonal multiple access
abstract: A user equipment for operating in a wireless network, wherein the wireless network utilizes a first number of resources for serving communicating UEs, comprises a wireless interface for communicating in the wireless network; and a controller configured for selecting, for communicating in the wireless network, from a second number of predefined subsets of the first number of resources, at least one subset. The second number is larger than the first number and the second number of predefined subsets is based on a mapping of the first number of resources into the second number of subsets using an Euler-square mapping.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A user equipment (50) (UE) for operating in a wireless network (80; 100; 150), wherein the wireless network (80; 100; 150) utilizes a first number of resources (52) for serving communicating UEs, the UE comprising: a wireless interface (54) for communicating in the wireless network (80; 100; 150); a controller (56) configured for selecting, for communicating in the wireless network (80; 100; 150), from a second number of predefined subsets (58) of the first number of resources (52), at least one subset; wherein the second number is larger than the first number and wherein the second number of predefined subsets (58) is based on a mapping of the first number of resources (52) into the second number of subsets (58). 2. The UE of claim 1 , wherein the second number of predefined subsets (58) is based on a mapping of the first number of resources (52) into the second number of subsets (58) using an Euler square-based mapping. 3. The UE of claim 1 or 2, wherein the Euler square mapping is representable as a matrix (F(n, k)) having a structure F(n, k), in which n Â· k is the first number of resources (52) and in which n 2 is the second number of subsets (58), wherein the matrix (F(n, k)) is structured so as to comprise a number of n entries indicating a use of resource elements in each row and so as to comprise number of k entries indicating a use of resource elements in each column. 4. The UE of claim 3, wherein the entries are arranged in the matrix (F(n, k)) so as to comprise a maximum spreading. 5. The UE of one of previous claims, wherein resources (52) of a first subset (58i) of the second number of predefined subsets is non-orthogonal with respect to a second subset (58 2 ) of the second number of predefined subsets. 6. The UE of one of previous claims, wherein the UE is configured for receiving, from a network controller (85) a selection information, wherein the selection information indicates a subset of the second number of subsets (58); wherein the controller is configured for selecting the at least one subset (58) in accordance with the selection information. 7. The UE of claim 6, wherein the selection information indicates at least one of 1. the allocated first set of resources or a property thereof; 2. an assignment of a specific Euler square matrix (F(n, k)) to be applied to the first set of resources; 3. a specific subset allocated to the UE for a grant-based access; and/or 4. a range of subsets to be used for communication for a grant-free access. 8. The UE of claim 7, wherein the UE is configured for receiving the selection information indicating the allocated first set of resources, indicating the assignment of the specific Euler square matrix (F(n, k)) and/or the indicating the range of subsets to be used for communication over a broadcast control channel; and/or wherein the UE is configured for receiving the selection information indicating the specific subset allocated to the UE over a user specific channel. 9. The UE of one of previous claims, wherein the controller (56) is configured for selecting the at least one subset (58) based on a grant-free access from the second number of predefined subsets (58) or a subset thereof indicated by a selection information. 10. The UE of one of previous claims, wherein the controller is configured for selecting the at least one subset (58) from a first version (58r58 32 ) of the second number of predefined subsets (58) during a first instance of time and from a second version (58i- 5836) of the second number of predefined subsets (58) during a second instance of time, wherein the first version (581-58 32 ) and the second version (58 3Q ) differ in view of a count of the second number and/or in view of a number of resources (52) contained in the subset (58). 11. The UE of one of previous claims, wherein the UE is configured for selecting at least two subsets (58) for communication. 12. A base station (BS) (85) for operating at least a cell (106) of a wireless (80; 100; 150) network such that the wireless network (80; 100; 150) utilizes a first number of resources (52) for serving communicating UEs (50), the base station (85) comprising: a wireless interface (87) for communicating in the wireless network (80; 100; 150); wherein the base station (85) is configured for operating the wireless network (80; 100; 150) so as to support communication of the UEs (50) by use of at least one subset (58) from a second number of predefined subsets of the first number of resources (52); wherein the second number is larger than the first number and wherein the second number of predefined subsets (58) is based on a mapping of the first number of resources (52) into the second number of subsets (58). 13. The base station of 12, wherein the mapping is based on an Euler square-based mapping. 14. The base station of claim 12 or 13, wherein the BS is configured for: monitoring a number of communicating UEs (50) served by the BS (85); adapting the second plurality of subsets (58) in view of a count of the subsets and/or in view of a number of resources (52) contained in the subsets so as to obtain a second version (58i-583b) of the second plurality; and operating at least the cell of the wireless network based on second version (58 58 36 ) of the second plurality based on the number of communicating UEs. 15. The base station of one of claims 12 to 14, wherein the first number of resource elements (52) is orthogonal with respect to each other, wherein the BS is configured for: 

 monitoring a number of communicating UEs (50) served by the BS; and serving the UEs (50) so as to use the orthogonal resources when the number of UE is below a number threshold; and for serving the UE so as to use the second plurality of subsets (58) when the number of UE is at least the number threshold or above the threshold. 16. The base station of one of claims 12 to 15, wherein the base station is configured for transmitting, to at least one UE a selection information indicating a subset to be used by the at least one UE, wherein the selection information indicates at least one of . the allocated first set of resources or a property thereof; 2. an assignment of a specific Euler square matrix (F(n, k)) to be applied to the first set of resources; 3. a specific subset allocated to the UE for a grant-based access; and/or 4. a range of subsets to be used for communication for a grant-free access. 17. The base station of claim 16, wherein the base station is configured for transmitting the selection information indicating the range of subsets to be used for communication for a grant-free access for a group of UEs or associated to the cell of the wireless network. 18. The base station of one of claims 12 to 17, wherein the base station is configured for allocating the first number of resources to the second number of subsets during a first instance of time and for allocating a second, reduced number of resources to the same number of subsets during a second instance of time. 19. Network (80) comprising: at least one UE (50) according to one of claims 1 to 11 ; at least one base station (85) according to one of claims 12 to 18. 20. Method for operating a user equipment (UE) (50) in a wireless network (80; 100; 150), wherein the wireless network (80; 100; 150) utilizes a first number of resources (52) for serving communicating UEs, the method comprising: 

 communicating in the wireless network (80; 100; 150); selecting, for communicating in the wireless network (80; 100; 50), from a second number of predefined subsets (58) of the first number of resources (52), at least one subset (58); wherein the second number is larger than the first number and wherein the second number of predefined subsets (58) is based on a mapping of the first number of resources (52) into the second number of subsets (58). 21. Method for operating a base station (85) such that a wireless network (80; 100; 150) utilizes a first number of resources (52) for serving communicating UEs (50), the method comprising: communicating in the wireless network (80; 100; 150); and operating the wireless network (80; 100; 150) so as to support communication of the UEs (50) by use of at least one subset (58) from a second number of predefined subsets (58) of the first number of resources (52); wherein the second number is larger than the first number and wherein the second number of predefined subsets (58) is based on a mapping of the first number of resources (52) into the second number of subsets (58). 22. A computer program product comprising instructions which, when the program is executed by a computer, cause the computer to carry out the method of claim 20 or 21
